task_completion.description: Verify the osint_tools directory was created in the correct Talon user folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file explorer window, terminal, or code editor showing the path 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\osint_tools'.

task_completion.description: Verify osint.talon-list exists and contains the user.osint_category list with the required mappings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'osint.talon-list' open in a text editor. It should declare 'list: user.osint_category' and contain mappings for 'map', 'scholar', 'flight', and 'network'.

task_completion.description: Verify osint.talon exists and contains the investigate voice command.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'osint.talon' open in a text editor. It should contain the voice command syntax: 'investigate {user.osint_category} <user.text>'.

task_completion.description: Verify osint.py exists and defines the user.osint_search action.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'osint.py' open in a text editor. It should contain a Python function definition like 'def osint_search(category: str, query: str):' decorated with Talon's action class.

task_completion.description: Verify osint.py contains URL formatting logic for the four OSINT services and opens the browser.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at 'osint.py' for conditional logic (if/elif) handling OpenStreetMap, Google Scholar, FlightAware (uppercase/no spaces), and BGPView (no spaces), along with a command to open the URL (e.g., webbrowser.open).

task_completion.description: Verify osint.py appends an audit entry to the correct log file with the specified format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at 'osint.py' for file writing logic (e.g., open(..., 'a')) targeting 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\osint_audit_log.txt'. The written string should include a timestamp, category, and final URL.

integrity.description: Ensure the files are actually created and saved in the file system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the files appear in a file explorer pane or IDE sidebar (like VS Code), rather than just being typed into a terminal without saving.

integrity.description: Ensure the Python script contains actual logic and not just hardcoded mock outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that osint.py uses variables (category, query) and conditional statements to build the URLs dynamically, rather than hardcoding a single specific search.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent wrote the code to generate the log, rather than manually creating a fake log file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the Python code responsible for writing to osint_audit_log.txt. If the log file is shown but the Python code lacks the writing logic, it is a violation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target directory for Talon module: C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\osint_tools. Audit log file path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\osint_audit_log.txt.
